name: JQ Method
description: The JQ Method is an advanced Square-1 solving technique created by Jack Triton. It combines cubeshape solving with block building and an algorithmic finish, offering an alternative approach to the standard Vandenbergh or Lin methods. The method leverages efficient cubeshape algorithms, intuitive block building, and a streamlined algorithmic endgame.

steps.name: Cubeshape
steps.description: Transform the Square-1 from its scrambled shape back into a cube. This step uses cubeshape algorithms to bring the puzzle into a solvable cubic form.
tips: Learn all cubeshape cases for fast execution
Recognize shapes quickly by counting edge and corner pieces on each half
Some shapes can be solved in fewer slices with optimal algorithms

steps.name: Block Building
steps.description: Build blocks of correctly paired and positioned pieces using intuitive techniques. This phase organizes pieces into useful groupings that simplify the algorithmic finish.
tips: Look for pre-formed pairs and blocks after cubeshape
Use slice moves creatively to build multiple blocks
This step benefits greatly from practice and pattern recognition

steps.name: Algorithmic Finish
steps.description: Complete the solve using algorithms to handle edge permutation and parity. This step resolves all remaining pieces using known algorithm sets.
tips: Learn EP algorithms for fast finishes
Recognize parity early to plan the correct algorithm
Some cases can be combined for more efficient solutions

pros: Block building phase allows creative and efficient solving
Lower slice count than some traditional methods
Good for solvers who prefer intuitive middle phases
cons: Requires strong understanding of Square-1 mechanics
Block building step has high variability between solves
Fewer community resources compared to Vandenbergh
creator: Jack Triton
